reviews.review_title: One of the best platformers in VR
reviews.review_description: I've initially got this game on PCVR a long time ago but had played just the first level before putting it down. After finishing Max Mustard and loving it, I decided to give this game another go but now on standalone, and I'm glad I did because I think I end up liking this game even more than Max Mustard. It doesn't have the innovative mechanics of Max Mustard, but the platforming seems better, it's more challenging, and I think the enemies have more charm like Pixar characters. The first level is more like a tutorial so it's not as challenging and its environment is not as engaging, but following levels are much better. If Max Mustard takes heavy inspiration from Astrobot, then you can clearly see that this game takes inspiration from 3D Mario games. The smooth animations, the cool enemy style and expressions (like the bee and the bird), the interesting levels, all remind me of a Nintendo game, and it's super cool to see all of this take life in front of you in VR. The water level and the temple levels are especially impressive both graphically and in level design. I loved the final boss fight, and it was hard but in a very good way. The only two issues with this game is that it's a bit short, and that you can clearly see that the resolution was defined with the weaker Quest 2 processor in mind, but luckily the latter can be solved by using QGO or a similar program. After starting the game using QGO with its default profile for this game (HD+) the game looks as nice on my Quest 3 as its PCVR version. I think this is a mandatory game for people who like platformers.

This is a beautifully crafted game it makes you wanna play and for this Gigi brings memories i long forgotten. No waking dead monsters just pure poetic fun. Please give more a phenomenal games like this. Definitely an incredible game the characters the colors flawless. Thank you so very much for your creativity.

I followed this game closely during development and I know Paul Bettner (Playful CEO) aimed to make a classic 3D platformer that would become the Mario of VR and at least for me, they've done it. This was the first game I played when Rift CV1 launched and playing it again here flooded me with nostalgia and pleasant feelings from a time before the darkness came for us all. 
When a genuinely new technology comes to gaming the earliest games are filled with moments that really take advantage of what couldn't have been done anywhere else and Lucky's Tale has plenty of that. Peeking behind things to see hidden coins, aiming bomb throws just by looking, reaching down amd interacting with Lucky are all VR specific treats that show the developers were just as excited and awed by what was capable as we once were. 
VR researcher Michael Abrash is famous for saying "these are the good ol' days" back when it was all mew and development was more like the wild west. He was right and we knew it and at least for me, Lucky is the mascot of those good ol' days. I really hope Playful comes back to VR someday, but if they don't ay least we will have Lucky.
This game is awesome.  I truly wish Meta had more games like this.  Not everyone is interested in violent games you know...
Cute, colorful and wholesome platformer. Nice music and SFX .Great chill out game.

reviews.review_title: we need more platforming games like this
reviews.review_description: the controls are perfect and the graphics still look good for a game this old

Really enjoyed this to start with but I'm only 6% through and can't get past the Towers level, at this point I'm bored and really want to get further but can't, falling in the drink is just soul destroying having to restart evey time so losing interest, shame as its a good game but you have to be good
It has to be time for lucky’s tale 2

Great 3d platformer, really fun and very cute. Reminds me of Max Mustard or the 3d Mario games.

Your view moves along with the character (like you’re the camera in a 3d Mario game, but much slower) which was slightly dizzying at  the first. I got used to it very quickly and didn’t have any motion sickness

If you love Mario and wish you could jump into the world, this is the game for you! I love all the characters, actions and different play modes. I’m going through a second gameplay now and having just as much fun as the first time.

I’m a man in his 40s,I grew up in a time where platformers were easily the most popular games in existence. This genre will forever have a special place in my heart as they played a huge role in my childhood. Mario,Sonic,Earthworm Jim,Ratchet & Clank,Donkey Kong Country,Crash Bandicoot,etc were like magic as a kid. Lucky’s Tale is no different,it offers nostalgia in a more modern format. My first headset was PSVR1 and Astro Bot:Rescue Mission was one of the best games on PSVR1. Astro Bot is what shown me platformers can still be great in VR. The devs done a fantastic job bringing this to VR. if you are a fan of classic platformers,stop reading the reviews and go ahead and buy this gem
I'm unable to save any progress, can anybody help me with that?

reviews.review_title: A MUST HAVE for Mario Fans!
reviews.review_description:
Don’t write reviews but absolutely blown away how amazing this is!! 

If you’re a fan of Mario and Astro Bot then this is a MUST PLAY!

The music and colourful levels remind me so much of Mario Galaxy, don’t hesitate and just buy, you won’t be disappointed!

Got this years ago and it is my go to chill out game. It's engaging, fun, challenging platformer! Special spot on my library.. Ps I've played other platformers and I just don't feel them, luckys tale has the magic touch to. Keep me playing

Hit the English button, but still in different language. I uninstalled and reinstalled hoping to go to the very start again with language, but it doesn’t come up saying English. Don’t know the other language, so not very happy with it.

reviews.review_title: Great, but Not for the motion sick prone
reviews.review_description:
Love the game, graphics, cute characters, things to collect, everything! Would love to play more of it, but the camera moves with lucky, which to be fair is just like a mario game. However that doesn't translate as well in a vr environment for me. :( 
It would be awesome if there was an option to make the camera static like in Moss! That would be perfect and we could move the camera ourselves or something like in Alice down the rabbit hole. 
If that gets an update, I'll totally come back and play more!!

The game is so much fun and colorful but, I’m stuck, there’s a glitch on this one part that won’t let me jump across the water, it’s off center. I’ve done everything I can to fix the problem on my end, it’s so irritating because this is one of my favorite games and I’m almost at the end😪

It's a fun, cute, colorful game with great graphics and great for kids as well as adults for a bit of fun between heavier, more complicated games. Unfortunately, it's so frustrating to have to keep starting over and over from the beginning. There is no way to save your game, as far as I know. So every time you "die" or need to leave the game, you have to start all over again! Why can't you save your game?  If you could pick up where you left off, it would be much more enjoyable.
Game won't come on. Tried uninstalling it 50 times and nothing.

Was super excited to get this game for my son. It looks like it would be cute but it has started in French and all instructions and narration is in French. I don’t speak nor read French and have been trying on and off for a few hours to figure out what button to press in order to get to language controls, but I cannot seem to find it. I’ve gotten to the point of thinking that my only choice is to uninstall and reinstall to see if that fixes it. 🤷🏻‍♀️

Great game, but for us old folks (or really little kids), the game just gets too hard and it’s too much work. I’m not that coordinated and not a gamer, but love the character and worlds, and really want to keep playing. Could you add some way  to progress without dying all the time and constantly starting over? There are lots of people my age who want to get into VR, and there aren’t many games we can play. You’re missing a big market!😉

Firstly congratulations on making what I think is one of if not visually the best game on the quest 3. The playability is faultless. Everything about the game is sheer quality!
Are you planning on making a sequel to this game?  I really hope so.
Garbage

The game does not work in my device. It’s just all black an only hear the music. 
It doesn’t work!
Played it previously on Xbox and gotta say way more fun on the quest.

reviews.review_title: The more I play it the less I love it
reviews.review_description: Edit: the more I play this game, the more I am irked by it. The controls, the fact you die so easy and no "save" option in between levels; If you have to stop before you have finished a level, you're out of luck... when you quit then, you'll have to start from the beginning of the level. That really limits being able to play for short times in between work or other things. Too bad because the art is really cute

reviews.review_title: Love the game but keeps crashing
reviews.review_description:
It’s a great game but it keeps crashing and booting me out to the Oculus main menu. So when I log back in I have to restart the level. I have an Oculus 2 with more than enough free memory

Devs please help
This game is amazing for all the Mario Astrobot lovers. The controls and graphics are great and and lots of fun. Get it!
The game has potential but I could not play more than five minutes. This is the first game to do this.

Usually I don't write reviews (actually, this is the first one for the Oculus...) But it will be a"crime" no to do it on this occasion...
If you are an "OLD dinosaur gamer" like myself, you will fall in 💜 with this amazing game.
It's a combination of Crash bandicoot, Bubsy, Super Mario Rayman, and Spyro wrapped in a VR atmosphere...
The only"bad" thing I can write about is the difficulty ( could be a little bit harder from my point of view) and that the game is a little bit too short...
Besides that , everything is PERFECT!!  I had the time of my life playing it...
HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!💪🤙🎮
Fun game

This will blow your mind, it's like being in the game, the pictures do not capture the realistic view of being inside a 3D platform game, a must have for your library, fun and great looking on Oculus Quest 2!

This is an AMAZING way to play platforming games - I wish more games like this existed. Game itself is full of content to entertain for hours - suuuuper fun to physically look around you for secrets and hidden coins =D

I've watched multiple videos and read countless lists of "best games on Oculus" but no mention of this (which I find almost unacceptable - because this is easily top 3 for me)

If you're reading reviews to decide whether to buy this... take my advice and just do it. Well worth the purchase - you won't be sorry :)

Loved the visuals and game play but dropped one star bc I quickly got motion sickness. I would love to control the camera view. It adjusts on its own. One would think that’s a nice feature until you experience it and get nauseous. Otherwise, it’s incredible, beautiful, adorable and SO much fun :)
Definitely enjoyed playing! It gives a mario feel and looks extremely realistic!

After playing Super Lucky’s tale on console, I was excited to see this remaster come out on Quest. It’s pure fun for all ages, whether you’re young or reminiscing about the days of classic platformers. The team did a great job remastering it, and it’s super fun in the VR environment. A must play.
This is one of my favorite games!! LOVE it!

I wish this game had more levels!!! Great game, fun! Love the Mario Sonic feel of this game however it only lasted a weekend. VR must get more non first shooter games please!. I play Moss, Supernatural and fisherman's tale. Love these games but I am able to beat them in a day or two. SN is ongoing obviously but the games, I beat them in a day or so. Please more options like lucky to VR.

reviews.review_title: It's a 3D platformer come to real life
reviews.review_description: 10/10 God bless whoever made this game, my childhood dreams of being inside a game of thus level of cuteness has come true excellent platforming, awesome game play, nice story, adorable characters we need a sequel asap!

My daughter loves super lucky tale on the xbox, but this vr version is unbelievable good. The graphics are fantastic. Controls simple, but very smooth. If you love Mario type games and want to experience that in a vr world this is the game for you.
